articles.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
articles.summary: Folk musician Murphy Campbell discovered AI-generated covers of her songs uploaded to Spotify without her consent. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in copyright systems and the misuse of AI technology to impersonate artists.

articles.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
articles.summary: As AI becomes increasingly difficult to distinguish from human-created content, the article suggests labeling human-made work with verification marks similar to Fair Trade certifications to prove authenticity.

articles.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
articles.summary: Anthropic is blocking Claude users from using OpenClaw by requiring separate pay-as-you-go billing instead of subscription limits starting April 4th. This effectively makes the third-party tool significantly more expensive.

articles.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
articles.summary: Claude Code subscribers will face additional charges for using OpenClaw and other third-party tools with Anthropic's coding assistant.

articles.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
articles.summary: Anthropic dominates private market trading as the hottest stock, while OpenAI loses ground. SpaceX's upcoming IPO could significantly reshape the landscape for all private AI companies.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
articles.summary: OpenAI's COO Brad Lightcap shifts to lead special projects in an executive reshuffle. CMO Kate Rouch departs temporarily for cancer recovery, planning to return.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure that challenges AWS and Google Cloud by addressing limitations in legacy platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200/month for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign in San Francisco featuring AI tokens that decoded into a coding challenge, attracting thousands of engineer applicants.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of giving AI agents extensive permissions across applications and files.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Novel Rowhammer attacks on Nvidia GPUs allow malicious users to gain root control of shared cloud machines by exploiting memory bit-flip vulnerabilities in DRAM hardware.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Recent research shows quantum computers could break elliptic curve encryption with far fewer resources than previously thought, with one approach cracking 256-bit ECC in 10 days using 100x less overhead.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX filed to launch up to one million orbital data centers to support AI growth without environmental harm. Tech giants like Amazon and Google are also exploring space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from global conflicts threaten plastic costs, as plastics derive from petrochemicals. Supply chain disruptions could increase prices for American consumers.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Middle East tensions have driven fuel prices to over $4 per gallon in the US. Plastic production may face supply disruptions as petrochemical availability tightens due to oil bottlenecks near Iran.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a new frontier multimodal AI model capable of running on-device. The model combines advanced intelligence with local processing capabilities.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use AI, advancing how artificial intelligence can interact with digital systems and interfaces more effectively than previous generations.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception appears to be a computer vision or perception system, likely for autonomous applications. Without article content, a detailed summary cannot be provided.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development, exploring emerging trends and technological advancements that will shape the industry's evolution.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing efforts.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available for developers and farmers to advance sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for the Gemini API—Flex and Priority—to help developers balance cost and latency based on their needs.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling users to generate high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models yet, designed specifically for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live introduces improved voice model with enhanced precision and reduced latency for more natural and fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can cause harmful manipulation in finance and healthcare, developing new safety measures to protect people from these risks.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and claimed token-efficiency gains. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene details and lighting. The technology marks a major shift in graphics rendering beyond traditional upscaling methods.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math/science visualization.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: Kilo launched KiloClaw to govern autonomous agents and combat shadow AI deployments. The platform addresses employees bypassing official procurement and deploying agents on personal infrastructure, exposing enterprise data.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Best practices include enforcing strict access governance, encryption, and role-based permissions to protect against emerging attack surfaces.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizes AI development alongside quantum computing and biotechnology through 2030, focusing on high-performance chips, software, and new model architectures. Infrastructure investments in 5G+, 6G, and satellite systems will support AI deployment.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from Penn State and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by tackling localization, self-positioning, and path planning.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical AI model limitations.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering combined with parabolic extrapolation enables LLMs to function as theoretical collaborators, producing testable multiverse models for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Netflix AI Team Just Open-Sourced VOID: an AI Model That Erases Objects From Videos — Physics and All
articles.summary: Netflix's VOID model automatically removes objects from videos while preserving physics and interactions, eliminating the need for manual VFX work. The AI handles complex tasks like making objects fall naturally when a person is removed.

articles.title: How to Build Production-Ready Agentic Systems with Z.AI GLM-5 Using Thinking Mode, Tool Calling, Streaming, and Multi-Turn Workflows
articles.summary: Tutorial on building production-ready agentic systems using Z.AI's GLM-5 model, covering environment setup, streaming, thinking mode, tool calling, and multi-turn workflows for scalable AI applications.

articles.title: Google DeepMind’s Research Lets an LLM Rewrite Its Own Game Theory Algorithms — And It Outperformed the Experts
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve uses an LLM-powered evolutionary agent to automatically design game theory algorithms for multi-agent reinforcement learning, outperforming expert-designed baselines in imperfect-information games like poker.

articles.title: FTC reports a surge in $220M job fraud - here's how to vet listings, according to recruiters
articles.summary: The FTC reports $220M in job fraud losses. Recruiters advise vetting job offers carefully in today's competitive market to avoid scams.

articles.title: LG G6 vs. LG G5: I compared the flagship OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
articles.summary: Comparison of LG's G6 and G5 flagship OLED TVs reveals key differences to help consumers decide on upgrading between the two models.

articles.title: Is increasing VRAM finally worth it? I ran the numbers on my Windows 11 PC
articles.summary: Virtual RAM can boost PC performance when resources are limited, but it's not a replacement for physical RAM. Increasing VRAM may help, though physical memory remains more effective.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are becoming more visible and integrated into everyday applications. Key trends for 2026 include increased adoption and prominence of ML technologies beyond backend dashboards.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: Agentic AI systems implement state-managed interruptions to halt agent execution pipelines intentionally, enabling human oversight and approval gates in autonomous workflows.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference phases: prefill processes input prompts using attention, decode generates predictions token-by-token, and KV cache optimizes decode efficiency.

articles.title: Building a Python Workflow That Catches Bugs Before Production
articles.summary: The article discusses using modern development tools to detect software defects earlier in the Python development lifecycle, preventing bugs from reaching production environments.

articles.title: Building Robust Credit Scoring Models with Python
articles.summary: A practical guide on building robust credit scoring models using Python, focusing on measuring variable relationships for effective feature selection in credit assessment systems.

articles.title: DenseNet Paper Walkthrough: All Connected
articles.summary: DenseNet addresses the vanishing gradient problem in deep neural networks by enabling better weight updates during training, preventing model improvement degradation.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into physical industries like agriculture and manufacturing. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training environments.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local agentic AI, enabling efficient performance across devices from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, fast models bring on-device AI capabilities with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield. The cloud gaming service expands its library with fresh titles for streaming.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals enables evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions, expanding beyond single-turn assessment patterns with systematic evaluators for helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation model training using SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling and expanding context windows. This reduced training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables AI agents to browse the web safely by using AWS Network Firewall for domain-based filtering and access control within VPCs, protecting against unauthorized data access.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 accelerates vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages to match improved model throughput performance.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models launched to scale from data centers to edge devices, meeting growing deployment demands across NVIDIA infrastructure.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI inference latency has reached single-digit microseconds, enabling algorithmic trading systems to respond faster to market events. This advancement allows firms to compete in high-speed electronic markets using neural networks alongside traditional hardware solutions.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive social media ads across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. The work analyzes LLM behavior through feature attribution to make AI systems more trustworthy and safer.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven approach to design imaging systems that optimize how well noisy measurements distinguish between objects. Uses an encoder and noise model to quantify measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A novel RL algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
